theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 6: Vibrant Play (Adapted for Rural India)
theme_and_archetype.reasoning: A light theme is essential for outdoor/daylight legibility on budget smartphones. Archetype 6 (Vibrant Play) brings warmth and approachability for school students, while earthy undertones ground it in a familiar Indian context, replacing harsh tech-blues with warm clay, yellow, and teal.
theme_and_archetype.emotional_tone: Encouraging, accessible, clear, trustworthy, and tactile.

typography.font_strategy: Headings use Baloo 2 (or a rounded fallback like Nunito) for a friendly, approachable voice. Body text MUST use Hind (or system fonts like Noto Sans Devanagari/Tamil) to ensure flawless rendering of Hindi and Tamil scripts on low-end Android devices.
typography.accessibility_rules: Minimum body size should be text-base (16px) but visually text-lg is preferred for low-literacy users. High contrast against backgrounds is mandatory (WCAG AA minimum).

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: p-4, p-6, p-8 for containers. Generous breathing room.
layout_and_spacing.touch_targets: CRITICAL: All interactive elements (buttons, links, list items) MUST have a minimum height of 56px (preferably 64px) to accommodate developing motor skills and low-precision tapping.
layout_and_spacing.border_radius: rounded-2xl for main cards, rounded-full for buttons and badges.
layout_and_spacing.borders_and_shadows: Neo-brutalist Lite: Soft clay-like UI. 1px solid border (`border-[#023047]/10`) with a distinct, slightly offset solid shadow (e.g., `shadow-[0_4px_0_rgba(2,48,71,0.1)]`) to make buttons and cards feel physical and pressable.

buttons.primary: bg-[#E85D04] text-white rounded-full min-h-[56px] font-bold text-lg flex items-center justify-center w-full px-6
buttons.secondary: bg-[#F3F0E6] text-[#023047] rounded-full min-h-[56px] font-bold text-lg border-2 border-[#023047]/10 px-6
buttons.icon_button: p-4 rounded-full bg-[#FFB703]/20 text-[#E85D04] min-h-[56px] min-w-[56px] flex items-center justify-center

cards.course_card: bg-white p-4 rounded-2xl border border-[#023047]/10 shadow-sm flex flex-col gap-3 overflow-hidden
cards.quiz_card: bg-[#FFFDF7] p-6 rounded-2xl border-2 border-[#2A9D8F]/20 text-center

navigation.bottom_tab: Fixed bottom, bg-white border-t border-gray-200. 5 tabs. Icons MUST be large (min 28px). Text labels under icons are MANDATORY for low-literacy clarity.
navigation.header: Clean white bg, left-aligned Title, right-aligned Language Switcher (A/अ/அ).

inputs.text_input: min-h-[56px] bg-[#F3F0E6] border border-[#023047]/10 rounded-xl px-4 text-lg text-[#023047] placeholder:text-[#4A4E69]

feedback.progress_bar: h-3 rounded-full bg-gray-200 overflow-hidden. Fill color: #2A9D8F.
feedback.offline_badge: bg-[#023047] text-white px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-bold inline-flex items-center gap-1

icons.library_recommendation: @expo/vector-icons (Feather or Ionicons) or lucide-react (if using React Web)
icons.rules: Icons must be prominent. Do not use abstract/clever icons. Use literal metaphors: Book for courses, Chat Bubble for discussion, Chart for progress, Home for dashboard. Avoid emojis.

motion.strategy: Minimal and performant. Target devices may be low-end Androids.
motion.interactions: Use simple scaling on press (e.g., active:scale-[0.98]) and basic opacity cross-fades for screen transitions.

images.description: Two young girls sitting at a desk in a classroom, perfect for onboarding or auth screen background.
Two young boys sitting at a table, useful for community/chat empty states.
School supplies floating (3D aesthetic), useful for default course thumbnails.

media.rules: Never use pure white backgrounds behind images without a container. Always use soft radiuses (rounded-2xl) on image wrappers.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is an Expo React Native mobile app (or React Web App styled as Mobile First). Assume standard mobile layout paradigms (column direction, constrained widths).

CRITICAL: If building for React Native/Expo, use <View>, <Text>, <TouchableOpacity> with Nativewind or style objects. If building a mobile web app in standard React, ensure a strict mobile constraint (e.g., max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen).
All interactive elements MUST include data-testid (e.g., data-testid='login-button', data-testid='course-item') as required by the prompt.
Accessibility is non-negotiable. Add standard ARIA or React Native accessibility props (accessible={true}, accessibilityLabel) to all touchable elements.
Prioritize Vernacular: Ensure the Language Switcher (A/अ/அ) is extremely prominent in the header. Do not hide it deep in a settings menu.
Implement the UI with a 'Fat and Friendly' aesthetic. Large text (min text-base), large icons, generous spacing. Do not use standard dense corporate layouts.
For the Video Player, use a robust YouTube embed solution that works well on mobile.
Mock offline mode visually: Use badges like 'Available Offline' and download icon toggles on course cards.
For the Discussion tab, differentiate teacher messages visually (e.g., a subtle #FFB703 background tint or border) from student messages.
